commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.compiledCode: var p=Object.defineProperty;var e=(n,o)=>p(n,"name",{value:o,configurable:!0});import{jsx as a}from"react/jsx-runtime";const g={Sunny:"\u2600\uFE0F",Clear:"\u2600\uFE0F",Cloudy:"\u2601\uFE0F","Partly Cloudy":"\u2601\uFE0F",Rain:"\u{1F327}\uFE0F",Showers:"\u{1F327}\uFE0F",Rainy:"\u{1F327}\uFE0F",Thunderstorm:"\u26C8\uFE0F",Snow:"\u2744\uFE0F",Snowy:"\u2744\uFE0F",Mist:"\u{1F32B}\uFE0F",Fog:"\u{1F32B}\uFE0F"},h={C:"\xB0C",F:"\xB0F"},I="C",T={HUMIDITY_SUFFIX:"%",WIND_SPEED_UNIT:"km/h"};function w(n){const{temperature:o,humidity:r,windSpeed:s,condition:i,unit:t="C"}=n;return useMemo(()=>{const c=`${o}\xB0${t}`,u=`${r}%`,d=`${s} ${t==="C"?"km/h":"mph"}`,m={sunny:"\u2600\uFE0F",cloudy:"\u2601\uFE0F",rainy:"\u{1F327}\uFE0F",snowy:"\u2744\uFE0F",thunderstorm:"\u26C8\uFE0F"}[i.toLowerCase()]||"\u2601\uFE0F";return{formattedTemp:c,formattedHumidity:u,formattedWind:d,conditionIcon:m,tempUnit:t}},[o,r,s,i,t])}e(w,"useWeather");function C(n){return a(Container,{padding:"var(--ggui-spacing-md, 16px)",background:"var(--ggui-color-surface, #ffffff)"})}e(C,"ComponentUI");function f(n){const o=useComponent(n);return a(C,{...n,...o})}e(f,"Component");export{f as default};

evaluation.critique: This is an incomplete skeleton with critical implementation gaps: all sub-components are empty (TODO comments), the main UI container is unimplemented, and component imports have invalid TypeScript syntax (e.g., 'components/WeatherHeader' as both path and type). While the architecture shows promise with proper separation of concerns, design tokens are only partially used in one location, and there's no actual rendered content to evaluate hierarchy or polish.
